summary: DHH announces a new version of Instiki, a wiki application, that ships as a native OS X client. The app bundles Ruby 1.8 and all required libraries into a double-clickable package. He positions it as a demonstration of how web applications can blend with native GUI environments. He mentions plans for a future Windows version.
key_points: Instiki's new version ships as a native OS X application bundling Ruby 1.8 and all dependencies
Web applications can blend with native desktop GUIs to create cross-platform apps
Self-contained packaging (bundling runtime and libraries) enables simple double-click deployment
money_quotes: It's a nice demonstration of how web-applications can blend with the native GUI and serve as a platform for making quick multi-os apps
key_insight: Web applications bundled with their runtime can serve as a practical approach to building cross-platform desktop apps with native feel.
